disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chest tightness is non-exertional, non-radiating, reproduced by chest-wall pressure (costochondritis/tension); onset at rest after a trigger (against ACS)
trigger_question_examples: Is the chest pain brought on by exertion or spreading to your arm/jaw, or is it reproduced by pressing on your chest?
Did it start at rest after reading about it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's reproduced by pressing on my chest, doesn't spread to my arm/jaw, and started at rest after reading.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No pleuritic pain, calf swelling/pain, immobility, or haemoptysis; normal SpO2 (against PE); no pericardial features
trigger_question_examples: Any sharp pain on breathing, a swollen painful calf, recent travel/surgery, or coughing up blood?
Any pain worse lying flat or on deep breaths?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No sharp breathing pain, no leg swelling, no recent travel/surgery, no coughing up blood.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began on reading about the low-titre ANA/a mild antibody-positive autoimmune condition; hyperventilating with prior health anxiety
trigger_question_examples: When did this start relative to reading about the condition?
Does it feel like previous anxiety/panic?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started while I was researching it and it feels like my past health anxiety.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Are my heart and lungs failing? Do I need an ambulance?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen ACS, PE and autoimmune condition pericarditis; if the picture is non-exertional chest-wall-reproducible tightness with normal SpO2, low PE risk and no pericardial features, de-escalate a low-risk panic attack with paced breathing and an ACS/PE safety-net, and explain a low-titre ANA is common and benign — without dismissing or over-escalating.
